contents:
# South Korea Ministry of National Defense downplays USFK troop reduction speculation

## Overview
South Korea's Ministry of National Defense stated no discussions remain underway with the US on reducing US Forces Korea (USFK) troops following US President Trump's comments on potential cuts in Germany. Spokesperson Chung Binna emphasized the continued robust combined defense posture against North Korean aggression. The ministry aims to strengthen the US-ROK joint defense system through close consultations.

## Details
Chung Binna spoke during a regular Thursday briefing, confirming no talks on USFK reductions despite Trump's April 29 remarks. USFK maintains 28,500 troops with a primary mission to deter North Korea. Experts like Kim from a defense think tank noted the peninsula's strategic importance in US strategy against China, making cuts unlikely. Hong Min from the Korea Institute for National Unification highlighted US shifts to agile units over large ground forces. Defense Secretary Pete Hegseth testified that alliances like South Korea receive US favor for meeting defense spending commitments. South Korea pledged to raise defense spending to 3.5% of GDP and transfer wartime operational control soon.[1][2][5]

**Location**: Seoul, South Korea

## Source Context
The information comes from Twitter @southkoreapro, which covers South Korea defense, DPRK threats, US-ROK alliance. The reporting matches their typical area of focus.

## Significance
This development adds to the ongoing pattern of activity tracked through this source. Analysts following South Korea defense will likely watch for follow-up reporting.
